q: Stimulus is a detectable ___ in the internal or external environment.
Stimuli influence a temporary increase of ___ activity or response in the whole plant or any part of plant.
In the plant, there are two types of stimuli such as internal stimuli and ___ stimuli.
The responses to external stimuli or environmental changes that are produced by the working of various organs in a systematic and controlled manner are known as ___.
External stimuli in plants include water, gravity, light intensity and duration, chemicals, temperature and ___.
Internal stimuli or internal control in plants are chemicals called ___.
Plants lack a ___ like animals.
Coordination and response in plants are undertaken by plant hormones or plant growth regulators or ___.
The environmental stimuli to which plants respond include abiotic or ___ stress.
Examples of plant responses include the ___, responses to touch, responses to herbivory and responses to abiotic stress.
Growth can be defined as an ___ increase in size of an individual cell or organ or its parts.
The process of development occurs in an ordered fashion within a single diploid cell called ___.
Cells of the ___ phase are isodiametric, thin walled, and consist of large nucleus and dense cytoplasm.
Cells of the ___ phase are found around the meristematic cells and are highly vacuolated.
Cells of the ___ phase undergo structural and functional differentiation.
Optimum temperature for growth is ___.
___ maintains the turgidity of plants.
Oxygen is necessary for cellular respiration to produce ___ energy.
___ nutrition is raw material for synthesis of protoplasm as well as source of energy.
Growth regulators are manufactured by living ___.
Life cycle of an organism consists of a sequence of changes, which are collectively called ___.
A plant hormone is an ___ compound synthesized in minute quantities.
Auxin, gibberellin and cytokinin are grouped as growth ___.
Ethylene and abscisic acid can induce ___ and abscission as growth inhibitors.
Chemically, auxin is ___.
Auxin inhibits the elongation of primary roots but can stimulate the initiation of lateral and ___ roots.
Application of auxin to unpollinated pistil makes them develop seedless fruits or ___.
In younger parts, auxin ___ abscission.
Auxin initiates and promotes cell division in ___ cambium.
Gibberellins include ___ acid.
Gibberellins stimulate the synthesis of ___ enzymes like amylases and proteases.
Cytokinin is a basic hormone and ___ derivative.
Cytokinins induce ___ in plants.
___ is a rich source of cytokinins.
___ is the only gaseous phytohormone.
Ethylene is produced naturally in plants from amino acid ___.
Ethylene is used in post-harvest technology for commercial ___ of fruits.
Abscisic acid is also known as abscisin or ___.
Abscisic acid is known as ___ hormone.
Abscisic acid acts as ___ of all growth promoters.
The internal inhibition of germination of a normal or viable seed is called ___.
___ germination takes place above the ground.
___ germination is where the epicotyl elongates faster than hypocotyl.
Germination of seeds inside the fruit while still attached to the parent tree is called ___.
Effect or requirements of relative length of day and night on flowering is called ___.
___ plants are indeterminate plants whose floral initiation is independent of photoperiodism.
A hormone called ___ is synthesized in leaves and is responsible for initiation of flowering.
___ involves inhibition of stem elongation, differentiation of chloroplast, and accumulation of chlorophyll.
___ is a blue-green pigment containing covalently attached chromophore.
Coordination involves two main body systems: the nervous system and the ___ system.
Animals respond to stimuli to regulate the internal environment by ___.
The central nervous system is composed of the ___ and the spinal cord.
The forebrain comprises the cerebrum, thalamus and ___.
The hindbrain consists of the cerebellum, pons and ___.
The brain is protected by three layers of tough, elastic tissue called ___.
The ___ acts as the centres for intellect, learning and memory.
The ___ acts as "the great relay station" of the brain.
The ___ contains neurones that control blood pressure, heart rate, and body temperature.
The ___ is involved in processing information from sensory neurones in the eyes, ears, and nose.
The ___ is involved in the unconscious coordination of posture and reflexes.
Specialized sensors located within skeletal muscles and joints are called ___.
The ___ serves as a relay centre between the neurones of the right and left halves of the cerebrum.
The ___ coordinates many reflexes like the rate and depth of breathing.
The butterfly-shaped core of the spinal cord is made up of ___ matter.
The ___ nervous system is under the voluntary control and its neurones service the head, trunk, and limbs.
The ___ nerve has branches to the throat and larynx and connects to many internal organs.
The ___ nervous system is under automatic, or involuntary control.
Sudden, involuntary responses to certain stimuli are called ___.
Reflex arcs are simple connections of only ___ neurones to transmit messages.
The sympathetic neurones release a neurotransmitter called ___.
The parasympathetic nervous system uses the neurotransmitter ___ to control organ responses.
___ cells nourish the neurones, remove their wastes, and defend against infection.
___ are short, branching terminals of dendron that receive nerve impulses.
The ___ conducts impulses away from the cell body.
The axon is enclosed in a fatty, insulating layer called the ___.
___ neurones have several dendrites and a single axon.
The connection between two neurones, or a neurone and an effector, is called a ___.
Chemical messengers called ___ carry the neural signal from one neurone to another.
Inadequate production of ___ is linked to Alzheimer's disease.
___ regulates temperature and sensory perception and is involved in mood control.
___ act as natural painkillers in the brain.
Sense organs convert stimulus into electrical signals or ___ potential.
The ___ is the outermost coat of the eyeball, which is tough, fibrous and opaque.
The ___ is a transparent layer continuous with the sclera that refracts light rays.
The ___ contains a black pigment that absorbs light and prevents internal reflection.
The ___ changes the thickness of the lens during accommodation.
The iris contains ___ muscles and radial muscles.
The ___ is a round opening in the iris that allows light to enter.
___ cells are sensitive under low light and are responsible for vision at night.
___ cells are responsible for colour vision under conditions of high light intensity.
The ___ (Yellow spot) provides the clearest vision.
The ___ spot has no photoreceptors and is not sensitive to light.
___ humour is a clear watery fluid that maintains the internal pressure of the eye.
___ humour is a jelly-like substance that prevents collapse of the eye.
___ vision allows a person to judge the distance and depth of objects.
Focusing on objects at different distances is called ___.
Endocrine glands secrete chemical messengers called ___ directly into the bloodstream.
___ hormones, such as testosterone and cortisol, are lipid-based.
___ hormone (hGH) stimulates cell division, bone and muscle growth.
___ promotes the retention of water by the kidneys.
___ lowers blood calcium levels by inhibiting release of calcium from bone.
The adrenal medulla secretes ___ and norepinephrine.
___ lowers blood glucose levels and promotes formation of glycogen in the liver.
___ raises blood glucose levels by converting glycogen in the liver to glucose.
___ promotes growth of the uterine lining and prevents uterine muscle contractions.
In the nervous system, information is transmitted as a ___.
